← All projects Case study — P.01 · Healthcare · B2B SaaS

CareBridge Connect

Healthcare already documents everything — but families still feel blind. CareBridge translates clinical documentation into plain-language family updates, cutting nurse call burden while raising family trust.

Role Founder & Head Engineer Status Live Since Jan 2026

The problem, in numbers

  • 2–3 hours per nurse shift lost to routine family calls
  • 27% of families dissatisfied with communication quality
  • Only 29% of families report being informed of care changes

The target customer is the skilled nursing facility, not the family. Operators want lower staff burden, higher family satisfaction scores, and increased census through stronger family trust — and they hold the budget.

What it does

Event-based care timeline

A timestamped feed of care events — medications administered, vitals recorded, activities attended — surfaced automatically from existing facility documentation. Families see what’s happening without staff making a call.

AI-generated progress summaries

Daily and weekly summaries that identify trends in plain language. Instead of raw clinical notes, families read: “Mom had a great week — her appetite improved and she attended three group activities.”

Guardrailed AI family chat

Families ask questions 24/7 and receive accurate, safe responses drawn only from their loved one’s verified care record. The AI is constrained to retrieved data, preventing hallucination while absorbing overnight call volume.

The Vault & permissioned access

A secure hub for facility contacts, medication lists, insurance cards, and care directives — with granular role controls so administrators, care staff, and family members each see only what’s appropriate, fully audit-logged.

The pivot that made it a business

CareBridge began as GatherIn, a consumer caregiving app for families. Fifteen-plus customer discovery interviews surfaced a clear pattern: families wanted better communication but had no purchasing power. The budget holder was the facility operator, who needed a turnkey communication layer — not another app for families to download.

The willingness to follow the market signal, even when it means rebuilding, is what separates real product work from class projects.

The rebuild reoriented the product around facility workflows, PointClickCare integration, and staff-facing dashboards — while preserving the family-side experience that made the original concept compelling.

Compliance as the moat

Every query runs through an auditable retrieval layer: a classifier determines which data categories are relevant, only those fields are fetched (with an audit log entry), sanitized context goes to the LLM under strict system-prompt constraints, and responses are filtered against PHI patterns before delivery.

That operational complexity is defensible. Any developer can clone the frontend in a week; replicating a compliant data architecture takes months. I wrote up the full lesson in What I Learned Building HIPAA-Compliant AI.

Business value for facilities

  • Reduced call volume — AI chat and proactive updates absorb routine inquiries
  • Higher family satisfaction — families feel informed and in control
  • Better online reviews — trust translates to five-star ratings
  • Increased census — informed families recommend the facility
